milestone: Mandatory Sign-In + Versioned Cloud Sync + My Cloud Worlds
slice: A real, confirmed data-loss incident -- traced via the user's own console diagnostics after 'why am not seeing my forest adventure in builder as am seeing it in studio' -- found builder_projects (Builder Project Cloud Backup) was 'local-primary, cloud backup only, never a second read path' with zero protection against two tabs/sessions/devices silently overwriting each other's saves; the compiled, already-Published Theme still rendered correctly in Studio, but the editable Scenes/Places/Experiences behind it had silently reverted, and a compiled Theme cannot be reverse-compiled back into editable authoring data, so the lost content must be re-authored by hand (out of scope here). Three explicit user decisions implemented together. (1) Versioned, conflict-aware sync, reusing the existing builder_projects.updated_at column with zero schema migration -- js/services/projectSync.js's push(project,opts) gained an optional opts.expectedUpdatedAt performing a conditional update (chained .select('id') to tell a genuine write apart from a silent no-op, mirroring themeRepositoryClient.js's own reset()/deleteTheme() discipline); an empty affected-row result distinguishes a real conflict (someone else's save already moved the row -- reported as {ok:false,conflict:true,cloudUpdatedAt}, never overwritten) from a brand-new Project this device has never pushed (handled via insert); omitting opts keeps the exact original unconditional-upsert behaviour. New get(projectId) fetches the current cloud row, resolving null rather than throwing. openWorkspace() now runs a non-blocking _checkCloudFreshness() surfacing an explicit 'Newer Cloud Version Found' prompt (dismissible, no forced action) whenever the cloud row is newer than what this device last synced; the Workspace's cloud-sync badge gained a real 'conflict' state (a rejected push shows 'Cloud has newer changes,' clickable) with a disclosed force-overwrite escape hatch for the legitimate single-user-multi-tab case -- no automatic merge logic anywhere, every conflict is surfaced to a human. (2) A new 'My Cloud Worlds' Welcome-screen section (_renderCloudWorlds/_cloudWorldCard) lists every real cloud backup this signed-in account owns via the existing ProjectSync.list(), showing exactly one of Sync to This Device (no local match -- the real 'new machine' case) / Update Available -- Load It (local copy is stale) / a disabled Up to Date per row -- distinct from and in addition to the pre-existing orphan-repo-card mechanism, which is untouched. (3) Mandatory sign-in, scoped to World Builder only: themeRepositoryClient.js gained signUp(email,password) mirroring the existing signIn() exactly, never touching Studio's own signinless _ensureAuth() anonymous-session fallback (confirmed byte-identical via git diff). The old, dismissible _showSignInModal() is replaced by a real, non-dismissible #wb-signin-gate blocking the entire Welcome screen until getIdentity() reports signedIn:true (Sign In / Create Account, a mode-toggle form) -- a real boot-sequence bug was caught and fixed while wiring this: the app's own boot IIFE originally ended by calling renderMyWorlds() directly, bypassing the gate entirely on first page load; fixed to call the new _checkIdentityGate() instead. Explicitly does not retroactively migrate anything published under an earlier anonymous session -- only Worlds published going forward become genuinely cross-device browsable under a real identity, a decision the user made explicitly and declined an anonymous-to-permanent 'upgrade' alternative for. tools/world-builder/ (v1) is untouched. Verified via a Node-only unit test loading the real projectSync.js against a hand-built thenable-based fake Supabase client (13/13 -- first-push-via-insert, matching-expectedUpdatedAt success, stale-expectedUpdatedAt correctly reported as a conflict with the row never overwritten, unconditional-push behaviour unaffected, get()'s found/null cases) and two full-browser Playwright suites mocking ThemeRepositoryClient/ProjectSync via route interception (15/15: the gate blocking/unlocking via sign-up, a cloud-only World syncing to a real local copy, Update Available for a stale local copy, Sign Out re-locking the gate; and 8/8: Up to Date, the pre-open freshness prompt, the conflict badge and its force-overwrite resolution), zero console errors throughout. Both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2) pass unchanged; git diff --stat confirms zero changes to tools/world-builder/ (v1) or any Studio-side file, and no supabase/schema.sql change was needed since the existing updated_at column already suffices.
